gaps: No test covers DFU initiated via 'Update All' button after a PC-side downgrade (dual-path firmware state manipulation).
No test covers the scenario where the headset is simultaneously connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app AND connected to a PC via USB for firmware downgrade before initiating DFU from the app.
No test validates the 'retry' option (or lack thereof) after a DFU failure — the bug specifically calls out 'no retry option after failure' which is untested.
No test covers stale/incorrect firmware version state displayed after app restart following a failed DFU — the bug reports that version state does not refresh correctly.
No test covers the 'Update All' DFU flow for a headset + Charge Stand combination (most DFU tests focus on Voyager Free 60+ earbuds + charge case, not headset + charge stand).
No test covers DFU failure recovery lifecycle: app close → reopen → check update availability → app restart → check version state persistence.
No test covers intermittent DFU failures — all existing tests assume success or deterministic failure paths (e.g., active call blocking).
No test validates the Software Update tab state after a failed DFU attempt — specifically whether 'Update All' button reappears or remains absent.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-001: 'Update All' DFU after PC-side downgrade — headset connected to app during PC downgrade
steps: Connect headset to Poly Studio Mobile app via Bluetooth.
Connect headset to PC via USB cable.
On PC, use Poly Lens Desktop to perform a firmware downgrade on the headset (e.g., from v6.0 to v5.0).
While PC downgrade is in progress, keep Poly Studio Mobile app open and connected to the headset.
After PC downgrade completes, disconnect headset from PC USB.
In Poly Studio Mobile app, navigate to Device → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' button to initiate DFU.
Observe the DFU progress indicator.
If DFU fails mid-way (intermittent failure), observe the UI state after failure.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Update All' button should be present after PC downgrade. If DFU fails, the app should display a clear failure message with a 'Retry' option. The headset should remain discoverable and connectable after failure.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-002: No retry option after 'Update All' DFU failure — UI state validation
steps: Connect headset to Poly Studio Mobile app.
Connect headset to PC via USB and perform a firmware downgrade using Poly Lens Desktop.
Disconnect USB and return to Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to Device → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to start DFU.
Force an intermittent failure (e.g., move out of Bluetooth range during DFU, or trigger a call notification).
After DFU failure, observe the on-screen UI elements.
Check for presence of 'Retry', 'Try Again', or 'Update All' button.
Check for any error message text displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: The app should display a 'Retry' or 'Try Again' button after DFU failure. If no retry option is present, this is a bug. The error message should clearly indicate the failure reason.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-003: Stale firmware version displayed after app restart following failed DFU
steps: Connect headset to Poly Studio Mobile app.
Perform PC-side firmware downgrade while headset is connected to app (dual-path state manipulation).
Disconnect USB and initiate 'Update All' DFU from app.
Allow DFU to fail (intermittent failure).
After failure, force-close the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recents).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to Device → Device Info or Software Update.
Observe the displayed firmware version for the headset.
Compare displayed version with the actual firmware version on the headset (verify via PC Poly Lens Desktop).
generated_testcases.expected: The firmware version displayed in the app should accurately reflect the actual firmware version on the headset after restart. If the app shows a stale/incorrect version (e.g., the target version from the failed DFU instead of the actual downgraded version), this is a bug.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-004: 'Update All' DFU failure recovery lifecycle — app close → reopen → check update → app restart → version persistence
steps: Connect headset to Poly Studio Mobile app.
Perform PC-side firmware downgrade while headset is connected to app.
Disconnect USB and navigate to Device → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to start DFU.
Allow DFU to fail (intermittent failure).
Force-close the app.
Reopen the app and navigate to Device → Software Update.
Observe whether 'Update All' button is present or absent.
Force-close the app again.
Reopen the app and navigate to Device → Device Info.
Note the firmware version displayed.
Restart the phone (full reboot).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to Device → Software Update.
Observe the 'Update All' button state and firmware version display.
generated_testcases.expected: After each app restart, the 'Update All' button should reappear if an update is still available. The firmware version should consistently show the actual version on the headset. No stale state should persist across app restarts or phone reboot.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-005: 'Update All' DFU for headset + Charge Stand combination — dual-device DFU
steps: Connect headset (e.g., Voyager Focus 2) to Poly Studio Mobile app.
Ensure headset is docked on the Charge Stand.
Connect headset to PC via USB and perform a firmware downgrade using Poly Lens Desktop.
Disconnect USB from PC.
In Poly Studio Mobile app, navigate to Device → Software Update.
Observe the Software Update screen for both headset and Charge Stand entries.
Tap 'Update All' button.
Monitor DFU progress for both devices.
If DFU fails for either device, observe the UI state.
Force-close the app, reopen, and navigate back to Software Update.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Update All' button should be present and should attempt to update both headset and Charge Stand. If DFU fails for one device, the app should show which device failed and provide a retry option. After app restart, the update availability should be correctly reflected for both devices.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-006: Software Update tab state after failed DFU — 'Update All' button presence validation
steps: Connect headset to Poly Studio Mobile app.
Perform PC-side firmware downgrade while headset is connected to app.
Disconnect USB and navigate to Device → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to start DFU.
Allow DFU to fail (intermittent failure).
Observe the Software Update screen immediately after failure.
Note whether 'Update All' button is still present, replaced by a different button, or absent entirely.
Navigate away from Software Update to another screen (e.g., Audio Settings).
Navigate back to Device → Software Update.
Observe the 'Update All' button state.
Force-close the app, reopen, and navigate to Device → Software Update.
Observe the 'Update All' button state again.
generated_testcases.expected: After DFU failure, the 'Update All' button should either reappear or a 'Retry' button should be shown. The button should persist when navigating away and back. After app restart, if an update is still available, the 'Update All' button should be present. If the button is permanently absent after a failed DFU, this is a bug.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-007: Intermittent DFU failure during 'Update All' — Bluetooth disconnection mid-DFU
steps: Connect headset to Poly Studio Mobile app.
Perform PC-side firmware downgrade while headset is connected to app.
Disconnect USB and navigate to Device → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to start DFU.
During DFU progress (at ~30-50%), trigger a Bluetooth disconnection by moving the phone out of range or disabling Bluetooth on the phone.
Wait 30 seconds.
Re-enable Bluetooth and reconnect to the headset.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to Device → Software Update.
Observe the DFU state and 'Update All' button presence.
Force-close the app, reopen, and check the Software Update screen again.
generated_testcases.expected: After Bluetooth reconnection, the app should recognize the failed DFU and show the 'Update All' or 'Retry' button. The firmware version should reflect the actual state on the headset. No stale 'updating' state should persist.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-008: 'Update All' DFU failure — app backgrounding during failure state
steps: Connect headset to Poly Studio Mobile app.
Perform PC-side firmware downgrade while headset is connected to app.
Disconnect USB and navigate to Device → Software Update.
Tap 'Update All' to start DFU.
Allow DFU to fail (intermittent failure).
Without dismissing the failure screen, press the Home button to background the app.
Wait 10 seconds.
Reopen the app from the app switcher.
Observe the screen state.
Navigate to Device → Software Update.
Check for 'Update All' or 'Retry' button presence.
Force-close the app, reopen, and navigate to Device → Software Update again.
generated_testcases.expected: After backgrounding and reopening, the app should show the DFU failure state with a retry option. The 'Update All' button should be present after navigating to Software Update. After full app restart, the update availability should be correctly reflected.
